items.title: FCA publishes findings from financial adviser market survey
items.summary:
We have published findings from our Financial Adviser Survey. The findings provide an updated picture of how the UK financial advice market is evolving and what this means for firms, consumers and future growth.

The survey brings together responses from more than 4,100 financial advice firms; along

items.title: FCA calls on law firms and claims management companies to consider the position of their clients
items.summary:
We’ve no vested interest in setting up a motor finance redress scheme.

What matters to us is getting fair compensation for consumers as quickly as possible and supporting a healthy motor finance market for the future.That's what our scheme will do, and it's free for consumers to use.Learn more abou

items.title: Sapia agrees to pay more than £19m to WealthTek clients after failing to protect client money
items.summary:
Sapia has agreed to make a voluntary payment of £19,637,950 to WealthTek clients and the FCA has censured the firm.

Sapia began working with WealthTek in 2013 and later appointed it as one of its appointed representatives. This resulted in Sapia holding and being responsible for protecting client m

items.title: FCA spearheads global action to stop illegal finfluencers
items.summary:
The FCA has led international action to stop illegal finfluencers putting consumers' money at risk.

Seventeen regulators worldwide took part in the 'week of action' which included enforcement activity, consumer awareness campaigns, and educational programmes for finfluencers who want to act respons

items.title: FCA Board appoints 2 new members to the Regulatory Decisions Committee
items.summary:
The FCA Board appoints new members to decision-making committee.

items.title: FCA consults on changes to IPO research rules
items.summary:
The FCA is seeking views on proposals to change rules that govern the publication of research during the initial public offering (IPO) process.

The FCA is consulting on removing the requirement for a 7-day delay before connected research on an IPO can be published. It also consults on removing rule

items.title: Motor finance compensation scheme challenged
items.summary: Our scheme is the quickest, fairest and most efficient way to compensate consumers. items.title: From promise to practice: shaping open finance policy with our Smart Data Accelerator
